content.question: Would abolition or modification of 6/8-year tracks significantly reduce socioeconomic and Roma segregation given that geographic settlement patterns, church/private-school exemptions, and centralized exam infrastructure remain unchanged?
content.reduction_path: Model segregation indices under policy scenarios that separately adjust: (a) extended tracks only, (b) church/private autonomy, (c) geographic accessibility, (d) combinations; compare predicted outcomes to baseline
